sampling_note: Two replays per variant covering wins and timeouts plus one extra flank seed for route consistency: baseline=[1 win, 4 timeout], rush=[1 win T6, 8 timeout], turtle=[1 win T22, 8 timeout], flank=[1 win T8, 2 timeout, 15 win used to confirm flank route stability]. Rush and turtle share the same timeout seed list (8,19,41,50,55,69,83,85,87,93) — a strong signal that timeouts are determined by web-guard battle dice, not by strategy.

per_replay_scores.notes: Win at T9 (spider-web captured tick 151). Canonical floor capture chain (soap-dish T2 tick 33, towel-rack T4 tick 56, wall-crack T5 tick 69), wall-crack ladder up at T6, double-push at T8. Pathfinders lose web-guard (4-round attrition: pathfinders volley deletes elite u0031 in round 0 then attrites; queen kills mage u0023, scout u0025, archers u0027/u0028); vanguard-bravo cleans up. Composition diversity is now visible inside the second battle: potato-bug u0020 deals 2/4/3 vs queen u0029 across rounds (armor 3 means atk 9 - def 6 = 3-ish), footman u0019 occasionally lands 2-3, archers and mage land floor 1. Tactical-variety bumped to 2 because volley actually kills elites (u0031 round 0, u0030 round 1 of pathfinders fight) rather than just chipping.

Timeout. Identical opening to baseline-1 through T6. T7 pathfinders + vanguard-bravo each engage web-watch (T7 ticks 102, 115) and win cleanly. T8 web-guard double-push: pathfinders wiped in ONE round (rounds=1, 6 dead, 0 spider deaths) — this is the dice-cliff failure mode. T8 vanguard-bravo follows up but loses 5 ants and only kills u0030. T10 vanguard-alpha catches silk-line at north-wall and wins (T10 tick 161). From T11 onward, vanguard-alpha alive but never redeploys; 89 turn-start events with only queen-ultimate-charged events (queen ult hits 100 at T19 and stays there). Outcome 0 — the failure has the same shape as iteration-0: pathfinders hit a bad-roll round 0 wipe, no recovery.

Win at T6 (spider-web captured tick 83). All three field parties climb-bypass to ceiling at T0 (pathfinders floor:1,1->ceiling:1,1; vanguard-bravo floor:0,1->ceiling:0,1) and walk diagonally to (9,9) by T5. NO ground POSTs ever captured, vanguard-alpha appears not to deploy. Three battles: pathfinders lose web-guard at T5 (canonical attrition), vanguard-bravo wins at T5 with 3 rounds, follow-up at T6 finishes queen with 2-round battle. Strongest decision-articulable in the sample — rush trades posts for tempo. Watchability genuine: events compressed into 6 turns (avg 99 events/run vs baseline 163), no dead air.

Timeout. Same climb-bypass opening as rush-1. T4 vanguard-bravo bumps into spider 'advance-scout' (u0045-48) on the ceiling and wins cleanly (tick 44). T5 pathfinders engage web-guard, lose canonically. T5 vanguard-bravo follows up, wins a costly 5-round fight (5 ants dead including footmen u0017/u0018/u0019). T6 pathfinders attempt web-watch and get wiped in 1 round (rounds=1, 6 ants dead, 0 spider). T6 surviving 1-body vanguard-bravo (potato-bug only) attempts web-guard and loses to queen+full elites. From T7 onward all field forces dead, identical 90-turn dead-air tail as baseline. Loss is articulable (advance-scout cost was real, web-watch wipe was crisp) but the no-redeployment failure mode is the same.

Win at T22. Vanguard-alpha solos floor capture chain (soap-dish T2 tick 16, towel-rack T4 tick 27, wall-crack T5 tick 34) then parries silk-line at north-wall T9 with zero ant casualties (best single fight in sample). Pathfinders + vanguard-bravo idle at storm-drain through T15, then march to ceiling and execute canonical T21 double-push. Web-guard fight at T21 (pathfinders lose) → T21 vanguard-bravo wins 5-round attrition → T22 vanguard-bravo wins again. Notable: between T10 and T20 there are 11 consecutive turns averaging 2-8 events each, of which queen-ult-charge alone accounts for the majority — pure dead air. Long-siege payoff is real but the middle is unwatchable.

Timeout. Same opening as turtle-1: vanguard-alpha solos floor capture chain, parries silk-line T9. From T10 onward the build-up phase runs LONGER than turtle-1 because pathfinders / vanguard-bravo only start moving at T16. Battles at T20 (vanguard-bravo vs advance-scout, win), T21 (pathfinders vs web-guard, lose canonical), T21 (vanguard-bravo wins 5-round web-guard battle leaving 1 archer + potato-bug), T22 vanguard-bravo retries web-guard and LOSES to queen — same dice-cliff as baseline-4. After T22 all field force dead, 78-turn dead-air tail. Outcome 1 (the long siege earns some credibility before the dice cliff).

Win at T8 (spider-web tick 123). FLANK IS NOW GENUINELY DIFFERENT. Three parties take three distinct geographic routes: (a) pathfinders walk west edge x=0 (floor:1,1->1,2->0,2->0,3->...->0,9), climb-bypass into ceiling at (0,9), then walk along ceiling y=9 east to (9,9); (b) vanguard-bravo walks east edge y=0 (floor:0,1->0,0->1,0->...->9,0), climb-bypass into ceiling at (9,0), walks along ceiling x=9 south to (9,9); (c) vanguard-alpha takes canonical floor route soap-dish/towel-rack and uses wall-crack ladder. Pathfinders and vanguard-bravo converge on (9,9) from OPPOSITE corners — exactly the corner-flank routing the iter-1 rewrite promised. Captures still happen (vanguard-alpha takes soap-dish T2 tick 34, towel-rack T4 tick 51, wall-crack T5 tick 64) but driven by a single party. Two battles: pathfinders lose web-guard T6, vanguard-bravo wins T7 + T8.

Timeout. Same flank opening as flank-1 (pathfinders west edge, vanguard-bravo east edge). T6 pathfinders engage web-guard and lose canonically (4 ants dead: mage u0023, scout u0025, archers u0027/u0028; 2 elites dead: u0030/u0031). T7 surviving pathfinders re-engage WEB-WATCH (this is new — flank's geometry brings pathfinders into web-watch's range) and get wiped (rounds=1, 6 ants dead, 0 spider). T7 vanguard-bravo wins web-guard. T8 vanguard-bravo wins again. T9 vanguard-bravo loses third web-guard attempt (rounds=3, 6 ants dead, 4 elites dead). 5 distinct battles in this seed — highest variety in the sample. Flavor still 0 (no jelly, no queen ult). 91-turn dead-air tail.

findings.observation: All four variants are now ABOVE the [65,80] band: baseline 84%, rush 90%, turtle 90%, flank 95%. The auto-tuner overshot. The iter-1 prompt notes the integer stat-step search bottoms out at α=0.406->84% vs α=0.418->51% (one HP step on spider-queen flips the cliff). Within the sampled timeouts, the failure mode is ALWAYS the same: pathfinders hit a 1-round web-guard wipe (baseline-4, rush-8, flank-2 all show rounds=1 and 6 ants dead vs 0 spider deaths). With queen at HP 41 and ant heavy-melee dealing 2-4 vs queen, vanguard-bravo wins web-guard 90-95% of the time — the cliff is on the queen's HP, not on broader balance. Re-tightening the band requires either bumping queen HP back up (queen 41->44 should land near 75% baseline, by linear interpolation between the two tuner endpoints) OR a sub-integer effective HP via a queen ability that adds variance.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 6 (troop mechanics): bump spider-queen HP 41 -> 44 (3-step) AND give the queen a passive 'web-mend' that heals 2 HP at the start of each battle round while she has > 50% HP. The static HP bump alone would just shift the cliff; the per-round mend introduces noise around the cliff so a single bad-dice round no longer determines the outcome. Expected effect: baseline ~72%, rush ~80%, turtle ~78%, flank ~83% — all four within or close to band, and timeouts become contestable instead of binary.

findings.observation: BIG IMPROVEMENT vs iter-0 (was 0/3, now 2/3). With spider-web POST defensiveBonus dropped 4->2, effective queen defense (2+2)*1.5 = 6 instead of 9, and a strict damage ordering against the queen has emerged in the replays. baseline-1 vanguard-bravo battle, round-by-round: potato-bug u0020 deals {2, 4, 3}; footman u0019 deals {3, 1, 2}; footman u0017/u0018 deals {1, 1, 1, 1, 3, 1}; archer u0021 deals {1, 1, 1}; mage u0022 deals {1, 1, 1}. Heavy-melee (potato-bug, footman) is now strictly more valuable than archery against the queen — exactly the lever the iter-1 change promised. flank-1 confirms this: potato-bug round-0 ONE-SHOTS elite u0033 with damage 4. Remaining gap: archers and mage are still floor-1 vs queen, so 'archer-heavy' compositions (pathfinders) still functionally do nothing to the queen. Pathfinders' role has effectively become 'volley once for elite kills, then die.' Two of three unit roles meaningfully matter; pure-archer comp is still dead.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 6 (troop mechanics): give ant-archer's 'volley' ability a queen-targeting bonus (e.g., +2 damage vs targets with 'leader' tag) so archer comps have a queen-specific tool, OR raise ant-archer base attack 4->5 so they at least clear the queen's defense floor by 1 sometimes. Either change makes pathfinders a viable queen-hunter party rather than a dispensable elite-deletion battery.

findings.observation: BIG IMPROVEMENT vs iter-0 (where flank was tick-for-tick identical to baseline). The rewritten flank AI now produces three GEOGRAPHICALLY DISTINCT routes per replay: pathfinders climb west-edge (x=0 column to floor:0,9 then climb-bypass to ceiling:0,9, walking ceiling y=9 east); vanguard-bravo climbs east-edge (y=0 row to floor:9,0 then climb-bypass to ceiling:9,0, walking ceiling x=9 south); vanguard-alpha takes canonical floor route through soap-dish/towel-rack/wall-crack. flank-1 captures match baseline-1 captures by tick (soap-dish T2, towel-rack T4, wall-crack T5) but the spider-web capture is 1 turn earlier (T8 tick 123 vs T9 tick 151) — flank's TWO ceiling-capable parties converge from opposite corners faster. Confirmed across flank-1 and flank-15 (consistent route). Rush is route-distinct (climb-bypass at home, no ground captures). Turtle is path-identical to baseline but time-shifted (15-turn delay before assault). All four variants now have DISTINGUISHABLE replay signatures.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 1 (spider AI): give web-watch a counter-push toward (4,9) on ceiling once ants own >= 3 POSTs, so flank's pincer movement gets contested in the middle of the ceiling rather than only at (9,9). This punishes the flank approach asymmetrically (pathfinders walking ceiling y=9 from west would meet web-watch first) and creates a flank-specific tactical wrinkle, separating flank's signature from a faster-baseline.

findings.observation: Wins remain credible (rush-1 = speed, turtle-1 = patient siege, baseline-1/flank-1 = coordinated multi-party assault). Losses are STILL not credible. baseline-4, rush-8, turtle-8, flank-2 all end with 80-90 consecutive turn-start events with only queen-ultimate-charged interleaved (queen ult reaches 100 around T19 in every timeout sampled and stays there for 80+ turns). The home-base party (vanguard-alpha or queen-guard) survives in baseline-4 / flank-2 but never redeploys; in rush-8 ALL field parties are dead at T7 with 93 dead-air turns to follow. To a reader the engine looks broken. Iter-0 had this issue and the suggested 'hold 4-of-5 POSTs' supplementary win condition was not adopted; reiterating because in EVERY sampled timeout, antPostsAtEnd is 4 (5 in some cases for flank/turtle).
findings.suggested_action: Lever 4 (scenario goal): add 'hold >=4 of 5 POSTs for 8 consecutive turns' as a supplementary ant win condition AND add 'all ant field parties wiped & no party can move' as an early-loss detector that ends the scenario at the wipe turn instead of running 80 dead turns. The win condition pulls plausibly-winning timeouts to wins (turtle-8 has 4 POSTs from T5 through T22 — would have won at T13 by that rule), and the early-loss detector ends rush-8 / baseline-4 cleanly when there's nothing left to do.

findings.observation: Volley/mend still fire as a fixed prologue: every web-guard engagement begins with 'pathfinders volley x3, mend, then attack' (baseline-1 ticks 102/103/105/106; rush-1 ticks 76/77/79/80). 42 ability events across the 6 sampled replays, all volley/mend, none situational. Royal Jelly never fires in any sampled replay. Queen ultimate never fires (charge maxes at 100 by T19 in every timeout sample). flank-2 stands out with 5 distinct battles (web-guard x3, web-watch x1, advance-scout-style x0) — that variety is generated by geometry, not by abilities. The rewritten flank AI added route variety but not ability variety.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 1 (spider AI) + Lever 4 (scenario goal): (a) give spider-queen a 'web-snare' triggered when she drops below 50% HP that immobilizes one ant unit for the rest of the fight — this makes ants want to RESERVE volley for the post-snare moment instead of front-loading. (b) make ant queen-ultimate auto-fire as a finishing AoE when ant queen ult >= 80 AND spider-queen is engaged in a battle with ants present — the unused 100-charge then has a destination.

findings.observation: Improvement vs iter-0 (was 1/3, now 2/3). Flank replays are genuinely interesting because of the corner-flank routing (pathfinders along ceiling y=9, vanguard-bravo along ceiling x=9). Composition diversity makes the second web-guard fight feel like ants are 'punching through' rather than chipping. Rush remains the watchability standout (6-turn finishes, no dead air). Remaining drag: (a) every web-guard fight still opens with the same volley-prologue; (b) timeouts produce 80+ turn-start runs with only queen-ult-charged events; (c) turtle middle (T10-T20) has 11 consecutive low-event turns. Reading 10 replays in a row is interesting through replay 4-5 then becomes repetitive when the same prologue and the same outcome dice-cliff repeat.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 1 + Lever 4 jointly: same as the tactical-variety suggestion. The single highest-leverage change to reduce repetition is making the queen behave differently mid-battle (web-snare or web-mend) so the round-by-round texture varies. The 'all-parties-wiped early-end' from outcome-credibility also fixes the worst part of watchability (the 80-turn dead-air tail) at zero AI cost.
